title: Log in as Admin and reach the dashboard
description: Verifies an Admin can log in through the Admin portal mode and is redirected to the dashboard on success.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the Admin portal mode
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email/identifier field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Verify the user lands on the dashboard

title: Protected route redirects unauthenticated users to login
description: Verifies an unauthenticated user attempting to access the dashboard is redirected to the login page.

steps.description: Navigate to /dashboard
Verify the user is on the login page

title: Start a session, mark attendance, and save
description: An engineer can open a session, start it, record attendance statuses, save, and see the updated attendance summary.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the sessions list
Select a session from the sessions list
Start the session
Mark at least one student as Present
Mark at least one student as Absent or Late
Save the attendance records
Verify an updated attendance summary is displayed

title: Admin control tower route is protected and requires authentication
description: A logged-out user attempting to access the admin control tower is redirected to login and cannot view pending registrations.

steps.description: Navigate to /control-tower/admin
Verify the user lands on the login page

title: Remember-me persists the signed-in session across reload
description: Verifies that enabling remember me keeps the user signed in after reloading the page and they still land on the dashboard.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the Admin portal mode
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email/identifier field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Toggle remember me on
Submit the login form
Reload the page
Verify the user still lands on the dashboard

title: Timetable route is protected and requires authentication
description: A logged-out user attempting to access the timetable is redirected to login and cannot view the timetable grid.

steps.description: Navigate to /timetable
Verify the user lands on the login page

title: Reject invalid credentials with an error toast
description: Verifies that submitting invalid login credentials shows an error toast and does not sign the user in.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the Admin portal mode
Fill invalid-user@example.com into the email/identifier field
Fill wrong-password into the password field
Submit the login form
Verify an error toast message is visible

title: Settings route is protected and requires authentication
description: A logged-out user attempting to access settings is redirected to login and cannot view system configuration.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Verify the user lands on the login page

title: Create a group with schedules and roster
description: An engineer can create a new group with weekly schedules and students and see it appear in the group list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open group management
Start creating a new group
Fill a unique group name into the group name field
Select a level for the group
Select a frequency for the group
Add a weekly schedule with a valid day, start time, and duration
Add at least one student to the group roster
Submit the group creation form
Verify the newly created group is listed in the groups list

title: View dashboard stats and upcoming sessions
description: Verifies a signed-in user can see dashboard statistics and an upcoming sessions list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the Admin portal mode
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email/identifier field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Verify dashboard statistics are displayed
Verify upcoming sessions are displayed

title: Admin can process a pending engineer by approving or denying
description: An administrator can approve or deny a pending engineer and the processed entry is removed from the pending list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the admin portal mode
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the admin control tower
Process a pending engineer by choosing either approve or deny on one list entry
Verify the processed pending engineer is no longer shown in the pending list

title: Navigate timetable weeks and open a session from the grid
description: An engineer can switch between timetable weeks and open a session from a timetable block to reach the session details page.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the timetable
Navigate to the next week in the timetable
Select a session block in the timetable grid
Verify the session detail view is displayed

title: View session details from the sessions list
description: An engineer can open a session from the sessions list and see the session’s core details displayed.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the sessions list
Select a session from the sessions list
Verify session details such as group, date, and status are displayed

title: Open a session from the dashboard upcoming list
description: Verifies a signed-in user can open a session from the dashboard upcoming sessions list and reach the session detail page.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the Admin portal mode
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email/identifier field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click a session card from the upcoming sessions list
Verify the session detail view is displayed

title: Prevent group creation when frequency conflicts with schedules
description: An engineer cannot create a group when the selected frequency does not match the configured schedules and is shown a validation error.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open group management
Start creating a new group
Fill a unique group name into the group name field
Select a frequency that will not match the number of weekly schedules you will configure
Add a different number of weekly schedules than the selected frequency using valid day, time, and duration values
Submit the group creation form
Verify a schedule or frequency validation error is visible

title: Timetable displays a weekly schedule grid after login
description: An engineer can access the timetable and see the weekly schedule grid load for the current week.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the timetable
Verify the weekly schedule grid is displayed

title: Edit an existing group’s details
description: An engineer can edit a group and see the updated details reflected in the groups list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open group management
Start creating a new group
Fill a unique group name into the group name field
Select a level for the group
Select a frequency for the group
Add a weekly schedule with a valid day, start time, and duration
Submit the group creation form
Open the created group for editing
Update the group name to a new unique name
Submit the group update form
Verify the groups list reflects the updated group information

title: Switching portal modes changes required identifier inputs
description: Verifies that switching between Admin and Student portal modes updates the login form inputs appropriately.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the Student portal mode
Fill a plausible student identifier into the studentId field
Fill a plausible engineer code into the engineerCode field
Select the Admin portal mode
Verify the email/identifier field for Admin mode is displayed

title: Admin can view the pending engineer registrations list
description: An administrator can open the admin control tower and see the pending registrations list area render.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the admin portal mode
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the admin control tower
Verify the pending engineer registrations list is displayed

title: Change attendance statuses before saving
description: An engineer can change a student’s attendance selection prior to saving and the final summary reflects the saved statuses.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the sessions list
Select a session from the sessions list
Start the session
Mark a student as Present
Change the same student’s attendance to Late
Save the attendance records
Verify the attendance summary reflects the final saved selections

title: Archive or delete a group from the list
description: An engineer can archive or delete a group and it is removed from the active groups list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open group management
Start creating a new group
Fill a unique group name into the group name field
Select a level for the group
Select a frequency for the group
Add a weekly schedule with a valid day, start time, and duration
Submit the group creation form
Archive or delete the created group
Verify the archived or deleted group no longer appears in the active groups list

title: Timetable session selection shows consistent session details
description: Selecting a timetable session block shows a session detail view that includes session context such as date/time and group association.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the timetable
Select a session block in the timetable grid
Verify the session detail view shows session context information (date/time and group)

title: Register a new engineer and return to login
description: Verifies a new engineer can register with valid details and is redirected to the login page after success.

steps.description: Navigate to /register
Fill Test Engineer into the name field
Fill test.engineer+1@example.com into the email field
Fill a valid password into the password field
Submit the registration form
Verify the user is redirected to the login page

title: Search and filter the groups list
description: An engineer can search the groups list and see results update to match the search criteria.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open group management
Start creating a new group
Fill a unique group name into the group name field
Select a level for the group
Select a frequency for the group
Add a weekly schedule with a valid day, start time, and duration
Submit the group creation form
Fill a distinctive substring of the created group name into the group search field
Verify the groups list shows the matching group and excludes non-matching groups

title: Admin can navigate from control tower to settings
description: An administrator can access system configuration by navigating from the admin control tower to the settings page.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the admin portal mode
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the admin control tower
Click the navigation link to settings
Verify the settings page is displayed

title: Send a chat message to a group
description: An engineer can select a group chat, send a message, and see it appended to the conversation history.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open chat
Select a chat group from the chat sidebar
Fill a unique message into the message input field
Send the message
Verify the sent message appears at the end of the chat history

title: Registration form blocks missing required fields
description: Verifies the registration form shows validation errors and prevents submission when required fields are missing.

steps.description: Navigate to /register
Fill test.engineer+2@example.com into the email field
Submit the registration form
Verify a form validation error is visible

title: Block group creation when required fields are missing
description: An engineer cannot submit group creation with missing required fields and sees form validation errors.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open group management
Start creating a new group
Submit the group creation form without filling required fields
Verify required-field validation errors are visible

title: Timetable week navigation returns to the original week
description: An engineer can move forward a week and then back to return to the original timetable view without losing access to session blocks.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the timetable
Navigate to the next week in the timetable
Navigate back to the previous week in the timetable
Verify the weekly schedule grid is displayed

title: Chat history loads when switching groups
description: An engineer can switch between chat groups and see the message history update for the selected group.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open chat
Select a first chat group from the chat sidebar
Select a second chat group from the chat sidebar
Verify the conversation history updates to reflect the newly selected group

title: Admin sees a stable empty state when there are no pending engineers
description: If there are no pending engineer registrations, the admin control tower shows an empty state instead of a broken list UI.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Select the admin portal mode
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the admin control tower
Verify an empty state is displayed for pending registrations

title: Presence indicators are shown in chat
description: An engineer can view participant presence indicators in a selected chat group.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open chat
Select a chat group from the chat sidebar
Verify presence indicators for chat participants are displayed

title: Timetable handles an empty week state
description: If the selected week has no sessions, the timetable shows an empty state instead of a broken or blank UI.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to the timetable
Navigate between weeks until a week with no session blocks is shown
Verify an empty state is displayed for the week

title: Scroll through chat message history
description: An engineer can scroll the chat history and continue to see messages without losing the current conversation context.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open chat
Select a chat group from the chat sidebar
Scroll upward within the chat history panel
Verify older messages in the conversation are visible

title: Prevent sending an empty chat message
description: An engineer cannot send an empty chat message and no new message is appended to the history.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open chat
Select a chat group from the chat sidebar
Attempt to send the message without entering any text
Verify no new message appears in the chat history
